test_description: This test case evaluates the system's ability to retrieve and synthesize information from multiple, separate document sections to answer a complex question.
question: [Fill] The pellicle membrane for a photomask is made of nanocrystalline graphene that includes _____ blank, which contributes to its enhanced uniformity and EUV transmittance, and the protective layer on the pellicle may include _____ blank to further improve mechanical strength and optical characteristics.

gold_context_sources.section_title: US20180149966A1 - 2. Description of Related Art - DETAILED DESCRIPTION
gold_context_sources.evidence: The pellicle membrane M10 may include nanocrystalline graphene having defects. The nanocrystalline graphene may include a plurality of nanoscale crystal grains. The crystal grains may include a 'two-dimensional (2D) carbon structure' having an aromatic ring structure. Defects included in the nanocrystalline graphene may include at least one selected from an sp3 carbon (C) atom, an oxygen (O) atom, a nitrogen (N) atom, and a carbon vacancy. The pellicle membrane M10 may have a thickness of about 100 nm or less and greater than 0 nm. The nanocrystalline graphene NG1 may have a transmittance of about 80% or more, or 90% or more, with respect to extreme ultraviolet (EUV) light. The pellicle membrane M10 may further include a protective layer on at least one surface of the pellicle membrane M10. The protective layer PL10 may include, for example, at least one selected from a carbon-based material, a metal chalcogenide-based material, a silicon derivative, and a metal oxide. The carbon-based material may include at least one selected from amorphous carbon, graphene, nano-graphite, carbon nanosheet, carbon nanotube, silicon carbide (SiC), and boron carbide (B4C). The metal chalcogenide-based material may include, for example, a transition metal dichalcogenide (TMD).

gold_context_sources.section_title: US20180149966A1 - 2. Description of Related Art - DETAILED DESCRIPTION
gold_context_sources.evidence: In the Raman spectrum of the nanocrystalline graphene, an intensity ratio of D peak to G peak, e.g., a D/G intensity ratio, may be about 0.5 or more. The D peak is generated by defects such as an sp3 carbon and a carbon vacancy. The pellicle membrane may have excellent thickness uniformity and surface uniformity with a surface roughness less than about 10 nm.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is [sp3 carbon, oxygen, nitrogen, carbon vacancy; carbon-based material such as amorphous carbon, graphene, or metal chalcogenide-based material such as transition metal dichalcogenide (TMD)]. Reasoning: According to the detailed description in US20180149966A1, the pellicle membrane is made of nanocrystalline graphene that inherently includes defects such as sp3 carbon atoms, oxygen atoms, nitrogen atoms, and carbon vacancies. These defects contribute to the nanocrystalline graphene's improved uniformity, EUV transmittance, and surface properties. Moreover, the pellicle membrane is further enhanced by a protective layer on at least one surface, which may include carbon-based materials (e.g., amorphous carbon, graphene) or metal chalcogenide-based materials (e.g., TMDs) that provide additional mechanical strength and optical performance improvements. Thus, filling in the blanks requires connecting the defect types in the nanocrystalline graphene with the materials used in the protective layer as specified in separate parts of the text.
